neurons.name: GLU
neurons.description: Gated Linear Unit. Fork input into two parallel paths. Gate path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim) then Sigmoid(). Value path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim). Combine gate and value with Multiply(). Project back with Linear(hidden_dim, dim).
neurons.lessons_learned: Created as Axon package. All pytest tests pass.

neurons.name: SwiGLU
neurons.description: SiLU-Gated Linear Unit (used in LLaMA, PaLM). Fork input into two parallel paths. Gate path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim) then SiLU(). Value path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim). Combine with Multiply(). Project back with Linear(hidden_dim, dim).

neurons.name: GeGLU
neurons.description: GELU-Gated Linear Unit. Fork input into two parallel paths. Gate path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim) then GELU(). Value path: Linear(dim, hidden_dim). Combine with Multiply(). Project back with Linear(hidden_dim, dim).

neurons.name: GatedFFN
neurons.description: Gated Feed-Forward Network with SiLU gating and dropout. Fork input into two parallel paths. Gate path: Linear(dim, expansion) then SiLU(). Value path: Linear(dim, expansion). Combine with Multiply(). Then Dropout(dropout_rate) and project back with Linear(expansion, dim).

neurons.name: Expert
neurons.description: Single expert block for Mixture of Experts architectures. Simple FFN pipeline: Linear(dim, expansion) then GELU() then Dropout(dropout_rate) then Linear(expansion, dim). Named as Expert for semantic clarity when composing into MoE systems.

neurons.name: PreNormResidual
neurons.description: Pre-normalization residual block. Fork input into skip and main paths. Main path: LayerNorm(dim) then Linear(dim, dim) then Dropout(dropout_rate). Add(main, skip) to produce output. Pre-norm places normalization BEFORE the transformation, which is the modern default (GPT-2+, ViT).

neurons.name: PostNormResidual
neurons.description: Post-normalization residual block. Fork input into skip and main paths. Main path: Linear(dim, dim) then Dropout(dropout_rate). Add(main, skip) then LayerNorm(dim) to produce output. Post-norm places normalization AFTER the residual addition (original Transformer style).

neurons.name: ResNetBasicBlock
neurons.description: ResNet basic residual block (He et al. 2015). Fork input into skip and conv paths. Conv path: Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, 1, 1) then BatchNorm(channels) then ReLU() then Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, 1, 1) then BatchNorm(channels). Add(conv_out, skip) then ReLU() to produce output. Uses kernel=3, stride=1, padding=1 to preserve spatial dimensions.

neurons.name: SEBlock
neurons.description: Squeeze-and-Excitation block (Hu et al. 2018). Fork input into features and attention paths. Attention path: GlobalAvgPool() then Flatten() then Linear(channels, channels / reduction) then ReLU() then Linear(channels / reduction, channels) then Sigmoid(). Multiply(features, attention) to produce channel-reweighted output. Note: the attention path learns per-channel importance weights from global context.

neurons.name: BottleneckBlock
neurons.description: ResNet bottleneck residual block. Fork input into skip and conv paths. Conv path: Conv2d(channels, bottleneck, 1, 1, 0) then BatchNorm(bottleneck) then ReLU() then Conv2d(bottleneck, bottleneck, 3, 1, 1) then BatchNorm(bottleneck) then ReLU() then Conv2d(bottleneck, channels, 1, 1, 0) then BatchNorm(channels). Add(conv_out, skip) then ReLU(). The 1x1 convs reduce and restore channels while 3x3 conv operates in the smaller bottleneck dimension.

neurons.name: ConvNeXtBlock
neurons.description: ConvNeXt block (Liu et al. 2022, modernized ResNet). Fork input into skip and main paths. Main path: DepthwiseConv(dim, dim, 7, 1, 3) then LayerNorm(dim) then Linear(dim, dim * expansion) then GELU() then Linear(dim * expansion, dim). Add(main, skip) to produce output. Note: DepthwiseConv may need special handling for channel-wise convolution with large 7x7 kernel.

neurons.name: ViTBlock
neurons.description: Vision Transformer block (Dosovitskiy et al. 2020). Two residual sub-blocks. First: Fork -> skip1 and attn_path. attn_path: LayerNorm(dim) then MultiHeadSelfAttention(dim, num_heads) then Dropout(dropout_rate) then attn_out. Add(skip1, attn_out) -> attn_residual. Second: Fork -> skip2 and ffn_path. ffn_path: LayerNorm(dim) then FFN(dim, expansion) then Dropout(dropout_rate) then ffn_out. Add(skip2, ffn_out) -> out. Same structure as TransformerBlock but intended for vision patch sequences.

neurons.name: MBConvBlock
neurons.description: Mobile Inverted Bottleneck block (EfficientNet). Fork input into skip and main. Main path: Conv2d(channels, channels * expansion, 1, 1, 0) then BatchNorm(channels * expansion) then SiLU() for expansion. Then DepthwiseConv(channels * expansion, channels * expansion, 3, 1, 1) then BatchNorm(channels * expansion) then SiLU() for depthwise. Then SEBlock(channels * expansion, reduction) for squeeze-excitation. Then Conv2d(channels * expansion, channels, 1, 1, 0) then BatchNorm(channels) for projection. Add(main, skip) for residual. Depends on SEBlock being created first.

neurons.name: PatchEmbedding
neurons.description: Image to patch embedding for Vision Transformers. Uses Conv2d(in_channels, embed_dim, patch_size, patch_size, 0) as a non-overlapping convolution to extract patches and project to embed_dim. Then Flatten() and Transpose() to reshape from [batch, embed_dim, num_patches_h, num_patches_w] to [batch, num_patches, embed_dim]. Note: output sequence length is (height/patch_size) * (width/patch_size).
